source.name: ICI — The US Retirement Market, First Quarter 2026 (Table 1)
source.note: Production display-only artifact (equity card retirement-asset-growth); not wired into GhostFlow Research Composite score (retirementFlowPressureProxy remains MOCK 58). Workbook: ret_26_q1_data.xls (https://www.ici.org/statistical-report/ret_26_q1_data.xls). Worksheet: Table 1 — US Total Retirement Assets. Units: billions USD in workbook; artifact trillions USD (÷1000). Quarter: 2026-Q1 (period end 2026-03-31). Release published 2026-06-18. Total retirement market assets = Table 1 total column row 2026:Q1 = 47,598 billion ($47.6T per release). IRA: column IRAs, row 2026:Q1 = 18,175 billion ($18.2T). DC: column DC plans1, row 2026:Q1 = 13,802 billion ($13.8T). Prior quarter total: Table 1 row 2025:Q4 = 48,798 billion ($48.8T). Prior year total: Table 1 row 2025:Q1 = 43,050 billion ($43.1T). QoQ growth % from ICI release headline (-2.5%) consistent with Table 1 sums. YoY growth % reconciled from artifact trillions (10.4%) consistent with Table 1 totals.

caveats: Structural retirement market assets from ICI Table 1 — not measured payroll contribution flow
Total assets are sum of Table 1 plan-type columns; components may not add due to rounding (ICI note)
Quarterly cadence — not comparable to weekly ETF issuance or daily vol inputs
mappingStatus not_final — no score mapping; retirementFlowPressureProxy remains MOCK 58
Display-only public card in equity signal grid; not merged into buildSnapshot or Research Composite
